detaileddescription.para: -1, represent ket state in physics
1, represent bra state in physics
0, only can be used in non-symmetry UniTensor
0, same as BD_REG
-1, same as BD_KET
1, same as BD_BRA

briefdescription.para: bond type

detaileddescription.para:
This is about the enumeration of the type of the object Bond.
For the UniTensor which is non-symmetry, the corresponding bondType will be set as bondType.BD_REG defaultly. You also can set the bondType as bondType.BD_KET or bondType.BD_BRA if you want to give the UniTensor more physics meaning.
For the UniTensor is symmetry, the corresponding bondType must be bondType.BD_KET or bondType.BD_BRA.
Please note that if you want to do the contraction for symmetric UniTensor, you must make sure that the two bonds you want to contract are one bondType.BD_KET and the other bondType.BD_BRA. Namely, you cannot do the contraction if two bonds are both bondType.BD_KET or both bondType.BD_BRA.
currently using gBD_* to indicate this is bond with new qnum structure!

briefdescription.para: Symmetry type.

detaileddescription.para: It is about the type of the Symmetry object The supported enumerations are as following:

briefdescription.para: fermionParity

detaileddescription.para: the parity of fermionis EVEN For bosons or an even number of fermions ODD For an even number of fermions

briefdescription.para: data on which devices.
UniTensor type.

detaileddescription.para: It is about the type of the UniTensor. The supported enumerations are as following:

simplesect.para: the type Sparse is deprecated. Use Block instead or create a LinOp.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< cytnx_uint64 > &shape, unsigned int dtype=Type.Double, int device=Device.cpu)
memberdef.name: zeros

briefdescription.para: Create a Tensor with all elements initialized to zero.

parameterdescription.para: the dtype of the Tensor. It can be any type defined in cytnx::Type
the device that the Tensor is put on. It can be any device defined in cytnx::Device

memberdef.argsstring: (std::initializer_list< cytnx_uint64 > shape, unsigned int dtype=Type.Double, int device=Device.cpu)
memberdef.name: zeros

memberdef.argsstring: (cytnx_uint64 Dim, unsigned int dtype=Type.Double, int device=Device.cpu)
memberdef.name: identity

briefdescription.para: Create a square rank-2 Tensor with the diagonal initialized to one and all other elements set to zero.

parameterdescription.para: the dtype of the Tensor. It can be any type defined in cytnx::Type
the device that the Tensor is put on. It can be any device defined in cytnx::Device

memberdef.argsstring: (cytnx_uint64 Dim, unsigned int dtype=Type.Double, int device=Device.cpu)
memberdef.name: eye

briefdescription.para: Create a square rank-2 Tensor with the diagonal initialized to one and all other elements set to zero.

detaileddescription.para:
Dim
the dimension of diagonal.
dtype
the dtype of the Tensor. It can be any type defined in cytnx::Type
device
the device that the Tensor is put on. It can be any device defined in cytnx::Device
[Tensor]
Note: This function is a alias of cytnx::identity().

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< cytnx_uint64 > &shape, unsigned int dtype=Type.Double, int device=Device.cpu)
memberdef.name: ones

briefdescription.para: Create a Tensor with all elements initialized to one.

parameterdescription.para: the dtype of the Tensor. It can be any type defined in cytnx::Type
the device that the Tensor is put on. It can be any device defined in cytnx::Device

memberdef.argsstring: (std::initializer_list< cytnx_uint64 > shape, unsigned int dtype=Type.Double, int device=Device.cpu)
memberdef.name: ones

briefdescription.para: Create a rank-1 Tensor with incremental unsigned integer elements in the range [0,Nelem).

detaileddescription.para:
The elements are 0, 1, 2, ..., Nelem - 1.
Nelem
number of incremental elements.
[Tensor]

memberdef.argsstring: (cytnx_double start, cytnx_double end, cytnx_double step=1, unsigned int dtype=Type.Double, int device=Device.cpu)
memberdef.name: arange

briefdescription.para: Create a rank-1 Tensor with incremental elements in the range [start,end) with given step-size step between elements.

detaileddescription.para:
The elements are start, start + step, start + 2 * step, ... The last element is the largest one that is smaller than end.
start
start value of the range.
end
end value of the range (exclusive).
step
step-size between subsequent elements in the range.
dtype
the data type of the Tensor, see cytnx::Type for more information.
device
the device type of the Tensor, see cytnx::Device for more information.
[Tensor]

memberdef.argsstring: (cytnx_double start, cytnx_double end, cytnx_uint64 Nelem, bool endpoint=true, unsigned int dtype=Type.Double, int device=Device.cpu)
memberdef.name: linspace

briefdescription.para: The addition operator between two UniTensor.

detaileddescription.para:
This is the addition function for UniTensor. It will call the linalg::Add(const cytnx::UniTensor &Lt, const cytnx::UniTensor &Rt) function.
Lt
The left UniTensor.
Rt
The right UniTensor.
[UniTensor] The result of the addition.
Lt and Rt must have the same shape.
linalg::Add(const cytnx::UniTensor &Lt, const cytnx::UniTensor &Rt)
internal/advanced: not a TN operation; python surface removed per #934 (decision record 2026-07-06). This note is the canonical rationale for every UniTensor-vs-UniTensor elementwise arithmetic entry point: the free operators in this header ('+', '-', '*', '/') and the UniTensor::Add/Sub/Mul/Div member functions (in-place and out-of-place). The python UniTensor dunders raise TypeError for the UniTensor-vs-UniTensor case (see pybind/unitensor_py.cpp, raise_unitensor_elementwise_removed) while the C++ layer remains. operator+ and operator- are load-bearing: src/linalg/Lanczos_Gnd_Ut.cpp and src/linalg/Lanczos_Exp.cpp use them as genuine Krylov-subspace vector-space arithmetic (axpy) inside the solvers backing linalg::Lanczos(method="Gnd") and linalg::Lanczos_Exp(). operator* (elementwise/Hadamard product) and operator/ (elementwise division) are basis-dependent, have no tensor-network meaning, and no in-tree C++ caller as of the audit; they are kept un-deprecated only because the removal task's scope was the python surface.

briefdescription.para: The addition operator between a template type and a UniTensor.

detaileddescription.para:
This is the addition function for UniTensor. It will call the linalg::Add(const T &lc, const cytnx::UniTensor &Rt) function.
lc
The left template type.
Rt
The right UniTensor.
[UniTensor] The result of the addition.
linalg::Add(const T &lc, const cytnx::UniTensor &Rt)

briefdescription.para: The addition operator between a UniTensor and a template type.

detaileddescription.para:
This is the addition function for UniTensor. It will call the linalg::Add(const cytnx::UniTensor &Lt, const T &rc) function.
Lt
The left UniTensor.
rc
The right template type.
[UniTensor] The result of the addition.
linalg::Add(const cytnx::UniTensor &Lt, const T &rc)

briefdescription.para: The subtraction operator between two UniTensor.

detaileddescription.para:
This is the subtraction function for UniTensor. It will call the linalg::Sub(const cytnx::UniTensor &Lt, const cytnx::UniTensor &Rt) function.
Lt
The left UniTensor.
Rt
The right UniTensor.
[UniTensor] The result of the subtraction.
Lt and Rt must have the same shape.
linalg::Sub(const cytnx::UniTensor &Lt, const cytnx::UniTensor &Rt)
internal/advanced: not a TN operation; python surface removed per #934; see operator+(const cytnx::UniTensor &Lt, const cytnx::UniTensor &Rt) for the full rationale.

briefdescription.para: The addition operator for Tensor.

detaileddescription.para:
This operator call linalg::Add(const Tensor &Lt, const Tensor &Rt) to perform the addition.
Lt
Left Tensor.
Rt
Right Tensor.
[Tensor] the result of addition.
Lt and Rt must have the same shape.
linalg::Add(const Tensor &Lt, const Tensor &Rt)

briefdescription.para: The division operator for Tensor.

detaileddescription.para:
This operator call linalg::Div(const Tensor &Lt, const Tensor &Rt) to perform the division.
Lt
Left Tensor.
Rt
Right Tensor.
[Tensor] the result of division.
linalg::Div(const Tensor &Lt, const Tensor &Rt)
The divisor cannot be zero.
Lt and Rt must have the same shape.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< UniTensor > &tensor_list_in, const std::vector< std::vector< cytnx_int64 > > &connect_list_in, const bool check_network=false, const bool optimize=false, std::vector< cytnx_int64 > cont_order=std::vector< cytnx_int64 >(), const std::vector< std::string > &out_labels=std::vector< std::string >())
memberdef.name: ncon

memberdef.argsstring: (const UniTensor &inL, const UniTensor &inR, const bool &cacheL=false, const bool &cacheR=false)
memberdef.name: Contract

briefdescription.para: Contract two UniTensor by tracing the ranks with common labels.

parameterdescription.para: if the inL should be contiguous align after calling
if the inR should be contiguous align after calling

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< UniTensor > &TNs, const std::string &order, const bool &optimal)
memberdef.name: Contract

briefdescription.para: Contract multiple UniTensor by tracing the ranks with common labels with pairwise operation.

detaileddescription.para:
TNs
the Tensors.
order
desired contraction order.
optimal
wheather to find the optimal contraction order automatically.
[UniTensor]
See also UniTensor.contract

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< UniTensor > &TNs, const std::string &order, const bool &optimal)
memberdef.name: Contracts

xrefdescription.para: This function is deprecated. Please use UniTensor Contract(const std::vector<UniTensor> &TNs, const std::string &order, const bool &optimal) instead.

memberdef.argsstring: (const UniTensor &in, const T &...args, const std::string &order, const bool &optimal)
memberdef.name: Contract

briefdescription.para: Contract multiple UniTensor by tracing the indices with common labels with pairwise operation.

detaileddescription.para:
in
the Tensors.
args
the Tensors.
[UniTensor]
See also UniTensor.contract

memberdef.argsstring: (const UniTensor &in, const T &...args, const std::string &order, const bool &optimal)
memberdef.name: Contracts

xrefdescription.para: This function is deprecated. Please use UniTensor Contract(const UniTensor &in, const T &...args, const std::string &order, const bool &optimal) instead.
